intro: Originally from Myanmar, now based in Newcastle. I'm finishing up my IT Management degree with a placement year at Honda Motor Europe under my belt.
paragraphs: At my placement, I learned that the best solutions come from understanding the problem properly—not just the technical side, but why it matters to the people affected. I built a  Business Intelligence dashboard from scratch, led an API project with international developers, and got comfortable being the person who explains what's happening when systems go wrong.

Before that, I led a 30-person team at a youth orgnisation through COVID-19 and political upheaval in Myanmar. That experience taught me more about leadership under pressure than any course could—turns out "pivoting to virtual" hits different when there's a coup happening outside.
Currently exploring how AI tools can be more context-aware (my dissertation topic), and writing occasionally on my Substack about navigating life as an international student.
strengths: Translating technical problems into clear business decisions
Building monitoring and reporting systems that people actually use
Staying calm when things break and coordinating the fix (I've had practice)
Working across teams and vendors to get things done
Spotting process inefficiencies and fixing them
Documenting things properly so knowledge doesn't walk out the door
